name: Pandas MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing AI agents with direct pandas DataFrame manipulation capabilities — loading CSV/Excel/JSON files into DataFrames, performing aggregations, filtering, joining, pivoting, and statistical analysis through MCP tool calls. Brings Python's most powerful data manipulation library into agent workflows without requiring code generation.
use_cases: Loading and analyzing CSV/Excel data files from data analysis agents
Performing aggregations and statistical summaries from analytics agents
Filtering and transforming datasets from data cleaning agents
Joining multiple data sources from data integration agents
not_for: Very large datasets (pandas is memory-bound — use Spark or DuckDB for big data)
Production database operations (pandas is for analysis, not OLTP)
Real-time streaming data (pandas operates on static snapshots)
best_when: An agent workflow needs to perform flexible data analysis on tabular data (CSV, Excel) without going through a database — pandas provides expressive data manipulation directly from local files.
avoid_when: Your data is already in a database (use database-specific MCPs for SQL queries) or exceeds memory capacity (use DuckDB or Spark for large-scale analytics).

auth.notes: No authentication — local data analysis tool. Access controlled by file system permissions.

pricing.notes: Free open source. Pandas is free open source. No external services required.

performance.rate_limits: Local — no rate limits; bounded by system memory and CPU

known_agent_gotchas: Large files can exhaust memory — implement file size limits before loading
pandas operations on large DataFrames can be slow — DuckDB is often faster for analytics
Agent-specified file paths could access any local file — validate paths if needed
pandas API has some breaking changes between major versions — check version compatibility
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local file access — can read any file accessible to the process. Validate file paths to prevent unauthorized file access. Data analyzed locally — no external transmission. Sensitive data in files stays local.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local file access. Restrict file path access if needed. No external data transmission. Data stays local.
